text.title: secrets-management Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for secrets-management as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Storing cloud provider access keys (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, GCP_SA_KEY) as CI/CD secrets.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Long-lived keys exposed in logs or to pipeline contributors grant persistent access. OIDC eliminates this risk entirely with no key to steal.

failure-mode.description: Storing secrets in ConfigMaps because they are easier to manage.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: ConfigMaps have no access control differentiation from regular config. Any pod that can read ConfigMaps in a namespace can read all secrets stored there.
